methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Heirloom Carbon is a Series B climate & energy company founded by Shashank Samala and Noah McQueen that has raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (KoBold Metals at $3.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M), supplemented by reference to 8 additional private comparables and 1 public company (LNZA) in the broader climate & energy universe.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling, using the $150M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $285M. Peer-median valuation across the three same-sector comparables yields $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B depending on stage proximity and tech maturity. The peer cohort reflects meaningful dispersion, reflecting heterogeneity in technology readiness and commercialization timelines within the climate tech sector.

These methods triangulate to a median valuation of $642M, bounded by a low of $242M and a high of $1.1B. The wide range reflects two primary uncertainties: the applicability of Series C peer valuations to a Series B stage company, and the degree to which Heirloom's specific technology and deployment timeline justify positioning relative to the peer median versus the lower or upper quartiles of the distribution.
